changelog: Fixes a false-positive 'Unsupported system: DNS server issues' repair warning found live on a fresh device 2026-07-06. Root cause: ensure_resilient_dns() (from cytech-15) restarts hassio_dns when it applies its plain-DNS/no-fallback config; Supervisor's own dns_server_failed health check can run in the few seconds right after that restart, before the DNS proxy's upstream connections have settled, and time out on a query that would have succeeded moments later. Because fallback is intentionally disabled, that one transient miss gets surfaced as a persistent unsupported-system banner that never clears on its own even though DNS is actually fine. Added clear_stale_dns_issue() in first_boot.sh: runs after every ensure_resilient_dns() call (both the maintenance-mode and initial-provisioning code paths), and only when Supervisor currently has an open dns_server-context issue, it re-verifies every configured DNS server still resolves Supervisor's own check hostname right now -- only dismissing the stale issue if they all pass, so it can never mask a real ongoing DNS failure. packages/cytech.yaml carries a comment-only bump in this release solely to force cytech_update.sh's PACKAGES_CHANGED restart trigger, so already-deployed devices self-heal via a normal Update Now tap rather than needing a manual restart or re-provisioning.
warnings: HA will restart automatically after this update (packages/cytech.yaml changed). On the next boot, clear_stale_dns_issue() re-checks and clears any stale DNS repair warning if resolution is currently working.
Devices not currently showing the DNS warning are unaffected -- this release is a no-op for them beyond the one restart.
